planContent:
# Redesign EditJournal to match Editor.jsx overlay UI ## Context EditJournal currently renders as a small absolute-positioned overlay **inside** each `.postcards-grid-item` card. The user wants it to render as a full-screen overlay modal at the `.profile-parent-container` level, matching the Editor.jsx layout and UI. ## Files to Modify 1. **`client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/ProfilePostCards/editJournal.jsx`** — Restructure JSX + classes to match Editor.jsx 2. **`client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/ProfilePostCards/profilepostcards.css`** — Replace old edit-journal styles with Editor-matching overlay styles 3. **`client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/ProfilePostCards/ProfilePostCards.jsx`** — Move EditJournal render out of map loop, use portal to `.profile-parent-container` ## Changes ### 1. ProfilePostCards.jsx — Portal rendering - Import `createPortal` from `react-dom` - Remove the two `` renders from inside the grid-view map (line 309-311) and list-view map (line 446-448) - Add a single EditJournal render **outside** both view loops, using `createPortal` to target `.profile-parent-container`: ```jsx {showEditor !== null && createPortal( , document.querySelector('.profile-parent-container') )} ``` ### 2. editJournal.jsx — Match Editor.jsx structure Restructure the component to follow the Editor's visual hierarchy. It already imports `editor.css` (line 3), so all Editor classes are available. **New JSX structure:** ```
← full overlay backdrop with blur ← centered modal card (720px, 80% height)
← right-aligned close button
← large serif title, bottom-border focus
← sticky toolbar
← bar loader during save
← scrollable editor content
← footer with save button
``` Key changes: - Wrap everything in `.editor-parent-container` (backdrop + blur overlay) - Use `.editor-container` for the centered modal card - Replace custom close button with `.editor-close-bttn-container` + `.editor-close-bttn` - Replace `.edit-joutnal-title-input` with `.content-title-input` (serif, bottom-border) - Add `.editor-footer` with save button styled as `.editor-save-bttn` - Remove the "Important Notes" section (unnecessary UI noise) - Keep the same save logic and LexicalComposer instance ### 3. profilepostcards.css — Clean up old styles Remove the old edit-journal-specific CSS (lines ~683-812): - `.edit-journal-container` and its rules - `.edit-journal-header-container`, `.edit-journal-header` - `.first-child-header-container`, `.second-child-header-container` - `.save-edit-button` - `.edit-journal-close-bttn` - `.edit-joutnal-title-input` - `.edit-content` - `.edit-journal-container .editor-shell` override All styling now comes from the existing `editor.css` classes. ## Verification 1. Open profile page, click edit on any post (grid or list view) 2. EditJournal should appear as full overlay covering the profile page (with backdrop blur), centered modal card matching the Editor's look 3. Close button, title input, toolbar, editor area, and save button should all match Editor.jsx styling 4. Editing and saving should still work correctly 5. Test on mobile viewport sizes

All three files look correct. Here's a summary of what was done:

### Changes Made

**1. `editJournal.jsx`** — Complete restructure to match Editor.jsx:
- Wrapped everything in `.editor-parent-container` (full-screen backdrop with blur, closes on backdrop click)
- Uses `motion.div` with `.editor-container` class for the centered modal card (720px, 80% height)
- Close button uses `.editor-close-bttn-container` + `.editor-close-bttn` (matching Editor)
- Title input uses `.content-title-input` class (serif font, bottom-border focus)
- Added `.editor-footer` with "Save edit" button styled as `.editor-save-bttn`
- Removed the "Important Notes" section and `showNotes` state
- Removed unused `useEffect` and `AnimatePresence` wrapper

**2. `ProfilePostCards.jsx`** — Portal rendering:
- Added `createPortal` import from `react-dom`
- Removed `<EditJournal>` from inside the grid-view map loop (was inline in each card)
- Removed `<EditJournal>` from inside the list-view map loop (also removed the `is-editing` class toggle)
- Added a single `<EditJournal>` render via `createPortal` targeting `.profile-parent-container`, outside both view loops

**3. `profilepostcards.css`** — Cleaned up old styles:
- Removed all edit-journal-specific CSS (~150 lines): `.edit-journal-container`, `.edit-journal-header-container`, `.edit-journal-header`, `.first-child-header-container`, `.second-child-header-container`, `.save-edit-button`, `.edit-journal-close-bttn`, `.edit-joutnal-title-input`, `.edit-content`, `.important-notes-container` and related rules
- Removed `.profile-postcards.is-editing` overrides
- Removed mobile override for `.edit-journal-container` and `.important-notes-container p`

input.prompt:
Investigate why clicking the italic button in the toolbar doesn't apply italic formatting to selected text in the Lexical editor.

Key files to explore:
1. `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/Toolbar.jsx` (or Toolbar.js) - Look at how the italic button is implemented, what command it dispatches, and whether there's a mousedown/click handler issue (e.g., the button click might be stealing focus from the editor, causing the selection to be lost before the FORMAT_TEXT_COMMAND is dispatched)
2. `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/editor.css` - Check if `.editor-text-italic` class exists and has proper styling

- Is `FORMAT_TEXT_COMMAND` being dispatched correctly for 'italic'?
- Is the CSS class `editor-text-italic` defined with `font-style: italic`?
- Are there any `e.preventDefault()` calls that might be missing?

Be thorough - read the full Toolbar component file and the editor CSS.

e.preventDefault()} onClick={() => applyTextFormat('italic')} className={getBtnClass(activeStates.italic)} title="Italic">
``` **Status: CORRECT** - The button uses: - `onMouseDown={(e) => e.preventDefault()}` - This preserves the editor selection when the button is clicked - `onClick={() => applyTextFormat('italic')}` - This properly dispatches the format command #### 2. **applyTextFormat Function (Toolbar.jsx, Lines 173-175)** ```jsx const applyTextFormat = (format) => { editor.dispatchCommand(FORMAT_TEXT_COMMAND, format) } ``` **Status: CORRECT** - The function correctly dispatches `FORMAT_TEXT_COMMAND` with the 'italic' parameter to the Lexical editor. #### 3. **CSS Styling (editor.css, Lines 5-7)** ```css .editor-text-italic { font-style: italic; } ``` **Status: CORRECT** - The CSS class is properly defined with `font-style: italic;` #### 4. **Active State Tracking (Toolbar.jsx, Lines 103-158)** The toolbar has an update listener that properly tracks italic state: ```jsx const italic = selection.hasFormat('italic'); ``` This correctly updates the `activeStates.italic` variable, which controls the button styling via: ```jsx className={getBtnClass(activeStates.italic)} ``` #### 5. **Comparison with Bold and Underline (Toolbar.jsx, Lines 338-346)** All three text formatting buttons use identical patterns: ```jsx // Bold (line 338)
e.preventDefault()} onClick={() => applyTextFormat('bold')} ... /> // Italic (line 341)
e.preventDefault()} onClick={() => applyTextFormat('italic')} ... /> // Underline (line 344)
e.preventDefault()} onClick={() => applyTextFormat('underline')} ... /> ``` All three are implemented identically, so if italic is broken, bold and underline should also be broken. #### 6. **ToolbarForEdit.jsx Verification** The alternative toolbar component (ToolbarForEdit.jsx, Line 106) also uses identical implementation: ```jsx {action: () => applyTextFormat('italic'), label: }, ``` And properly uses `onMouseDown` with `e.preventDefault()` and `e.stopPropagation()`: ```jsx
{e.stopPropagation(); e.preventDefault(); link.action()}} key={index} className="toolbar-bttns"> ``` ### Summary: **The italic button implementation is correct and follows all best practices:** - Uses `onMouseDown` with `e.preventDefault()` to prevent selection loss - Properly dispatches `FORMAT_TEXT_COMMAND` with 'italic' parameter - CSS class `editor-text-italic` is correctly defined - Active state tracking is properly implemented - No missing event handlers or preventDefault calls ### Possible Causes for User-Reported Issue: If the italic button isn't working despite correct implementation, the issue likely lies elsewhere: 1. **Lexical configuration** - Check if the Lexical composer/editor is properly initialized with text formatting support 2. **Editor state preservation** - Verify that the selection is being maintained when the button is clicked 3. **Browser-level issues** - CSS might be overridden or the font might not support italic rendering 4. **Lexical version** - There might be a version mismatch or compatibility issue with `FORMAT_TEXT_COMMAND` **File paths analyzed:** - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/Toolbar.jsx` - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/editor.css` - `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/ToolbarForEdit.jsx`

**Found it.** Line 9 in `index.css`:

```css
font-synthesis: none;
```

This is the root cause. Here's what's happening:

1. The `:root` sets `font-family: "Outfit", sans-serif` — **Outfit does NOT have an italic variant** (it's only loaded with weights, no `ital`).
2. The editor content (`.editor-paragraph`, `.editor-input`) **does not set its own `font-family`**, so it inherits **Outfit** from `:root`.
3. `font-synthesis: none` on `:root` tells the browser: "Do NOT synthesize faux italic for fonts that don't have a real italic variant."
4. So when Lexical applies `font-style: italic` via `.editor-text-italic`, the browser tries to use Outfit's italic face, which doesn't exist, and `font-synthesis: none` prevents it from faking one. Result: text looks identical to regular.

The fix is to set `font-family: var(--font-serif)` on the editor content area (`.editor-input` or `.editor-shell`), so editor text uses **Lora** (which IS loaded with italic variants). This also makes the editor text use a nice serif reading font rather than the UI font.

# Fix: Italic formatting not visually rendering in editor

## Context
When users select text and click the italic toolbar button (or toggle italic and start typing), the button correctly highlights as active but the **text doesn't visually appear italic**. Bold and underline have the same issue. Root cause identified.

## Root Cause
In `client/src/index.css` line 9, `:root` declares:
```css
font-synthesis: none;
```
Combined with:
- `:root` sets `font-family: "Outfit", sans-serif` (line 4)
- **Outfit is loaded WITHOUT italic variants** (only weights: `Outfit:wght@300;400;500;600;700`)
- The editor content areas (`.editor-input`, `.editor-paragraph`) inherit Outfit from `:root` — they have **no explicit font-family**

`font-synthesis: none` tells the browser not to synthesize faux italic/bold for fonts missing those variants. Since Outfit has no italic face, `font-style: italic` has no visible effect.

## Fix
**File:** `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/editor.css`

Add `font-family: var(--font-serif)` to `.editor-input` so editor text uses **Lora** (which IS loaded with italic variants: `Lora:ital,wght@0,400;0,500;0,600;0,700;1,400;1,500`).

```css
.editor-input {
    font-family: var(--font-serif);
}
```

This single rule fixes italic rendering in both Editor.jsx and editJournal.jsx because both use `.editor-input` via their `<ContentEditable className="editor-input" />`.

## Why this is the right fix
- Lora is already the project's designated reading/content font (`--font-serif`)
- The title input already uses `var(--font-serif)` — the body text should match
- Lora has proper italic glyphs loaded, so `font-style: italic` renders correctly
- No need to remove `font-synthesis: none` from `:root` (it's a good practice for UI text using Outfit)

## Verification
1. Open the editor (click "Write" or edit an existing post)
2. Type some text, select it, click the italic button — text should visually slant
3. Toggle italic on, start typing — new text should appear italic
4. Verify bold and underline also render correctly
5. Check the content viewer page to confirm saved italic text renders properly (ContentView.jsx already has its own theme with italic mapping)
